rateLimitNote: Figma MCP Starter-plan tool-call limit was hit after the first 3 successful calls (whoami, get_metadata for frame 24:3285, get_variable_defs). get_design_context, get_screenshot, and deeper get_metadata calls for the stacked-card/top-app-bar/FAB instances all returned 'tool call limit' errors. The data below is what we actually pulled; everything else is marked as an open question for the developer.

frame.name: Google Pixel 2 - 1
frame.breakpoint: mobile (matches Google Pixel 2 viewport, 411 logical px wide)

children.name: top-app-bar
children.notes: Standard Material 3 top app bar (64dp). Slight 1px x-offset suggests full-bleed across the frame. Master component not inspected (rate-limited).

children.name: stacked-card
children.notes: First stacked-card instance. Same master as the two below.

children.name: stacked-card
children.notes: Second stacked-card instance. Vertical gap from previous card: 614 - (99+480) = 35px.

children.name: stacked-card
children.notes: Third stacked-card instance. Vertical gap from previous card: 1129 - (614+480) = 35px. Card extends past frame height (1609 > 731) -> the frame is a scroll container, not the visible viewport.

children.name: FAB
children.notes: Floating action button: 56x56 surface with 24x24 icon at 16,16 inset. NOT a Figma component instance, just a frame -> risk #4 candidate. Position 331,651 = 24px from right edge, 24px from bottom edge of the visible 411x731 viewport (since 411-(331+56)=24 and 731-(651+56)=24). Treat as the standard FAB anchored bottom-right with 24px insets.

layoutInference.scroll: Children at y=99, 614, 1129 with 480px tall cards means content height >= 1609px against a 731px viewport -> page is vertically scrollable
layoutInference.horizontalPadding: Cards sit at x=25, are 360px wide. 25 + 360 + 25 (mirror) = 410, matches the 411 frame width within 1px. So horizontal page padding = 24-25px (likely 24dp Material side margin)
layoutInference.cardGap: 35px between cards. NOT on the 4/8/12/16/24/32 scale -> token gap (closest scale: 32). Recommend asking designer; provisional close as --app-feed-card-gap: 32px or 35px.
layoutInference.topOffset: First card y=99, top-app-bar ends at y=64 -> 35px clear below the app bar. Same 35px outlier.

tokenCensus.boundPercentage: 100% of observed values on the frame node itself reference published M3 variables. Bound rate INSIDE the stacked-card master is unknown (rate limited).

instances.name: stacked-card
instances.master: same master across 3 instances - reuse candidate -> ui/stacked-card

frames_not_instances.name: FAB
frames_not_instances.note: Pure frame, not a Figma component. Treat as a single <button mat-fab> in the page; do not create a ui/ wrapper.

openQuestionsFromExtraction: stacked-card internal structure (media area, title, supporting text, actions) was not inspected because get_design_context was rate-limited. Need to either upgrade the Figma plan or have the developer paste the master component's structure.
top-app-bar variant is unresolved (small / center-aligned / medium / large). 64dp height matches the small/center-aligned variants.
FAB icon glyph is unknown - 24x24 frame, no Material icon name extractable here.
Card vertical gap is 35px - not on the 4/8/12/16/24/32 scale. Confirm canonical value (likely 32 or 36).
No tablet/desktop frame in this URL - only mobile (411x731). Tablet/desktop adaptation strategy must come from the developer.
